Meta.title: Numbers — CFCC Reimbursements
Meta.description: Snap church receipts now, submit reimbursement claims later.

Home.profileNudge: Your <link>profile</link> needs a name and mailing address — both are printed on the reimbursement form.

SignIn.tagline: CFCC expense reimbursements — snap receipts now, file the claim later.
SignIn.popupBlocked: Your browser blocked the sign-in popup. If you opened this from inside another app, tap the menu and choose “Open in Safari” (or Chrome), then try again.
SignIn.embeddedBody: Google sign-in may not work in in-app browsers. If it doesn't, open this page in Safari or Chrome — tap the menu (⋯ or the share icon) and choose “Open in Safari”.
SignIn.notConfigured: No sign-in method is configured. Set FIREBASE_API_KEY, FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN and FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID in the environment.

Claims.title: Claims
Claims.subtitle: Draft claims need review; claims marked “Ready to submit” can be printed or sent for approval.
Claims.emptyBody: Select receipts on the <link>Receipts</link> page and hit “New Claim”.
Claims.counts: {items} items · {receipts, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}

Profile.title: Profile
Profile.subtitle: Printed onto the “Name” and “Address” lines of the reimbursement form.
Profile.dutiesNote: Step back from a duty for a while — while it's off, other members won't see you as an option.
Profile.dutyApprovalsOn: Members can pick you as the approver when they submit a claim.
Profile.dutyApprovalsOff: You're not taking new approval requests. Claims already assigned to you still wait for your decision.
Profile.dutyFinanceOn: You can see the finance queue and mark approved claims as paid.
Profile.dutyFinanceOff: The finance queue is hidden and you can't mark claims as paid.
Profile.dutyAdminOn: You can change members' roles, and admins can also manage settings and electronic signing.
Profile.dutyAdminOff: Role controls and admin pages are hidden until you turn this back on.
Profile.teamsBody: Team membership lets you view receipts filed under the team’s budget categories. Find them under <link>Search → My teams</link>.

PdfPreview.omitted: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — open the PDF receipt to view it.} other {# more pages not shown — open the PDF receipt to view them.}}

Shoebox.title: Receipts
Shoebox.intro: Add receipts as you go — snap a photo or upload a file. Select some when you're ready to file a claim.
Shoebox.restoredPhoto: Recovered from your last visit — this photo hadn't been saved yet.
Shoebox.selectedCount: {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} selected
Shoebox.selectPrompt: Select receipts below to start a claim
Shoebox.selectHint: Select a receipt first — click a ✓ below
Shoebox.readingCount: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
Shoebox.emptyBody: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to get started.
Shoebox.step1: <step>1. Snap.</step> Photograph receipts the moment you buy.
Shoebox.step2: <step>2. Batch.</step> Later, when you're ready, select receipts and hit New Claim.
Shoebox.step3: <step>3. Verify.</step> Check every row against the receipt. Fix, split, or exclude items.
Shoebox.step4: <step>4. Print.</step> Download the filled CFCC form with receipts attached, sign, and drop it off.
Shoebox.processedNote: Already on a generated claim. You can still pick it if part of the purchase belongs on another claim.
Shoebox.prepareHintLocal: Uploads when you save. You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.prepareHintUploaded: You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.quotaRingAria: Retrying in about {seconds, plural, one {# second} other {# seconds}}
Shoebox.openGoneToast: That receipt is no longer in your Receipts.

Viewer.previewUnavailable: Preview unavailable — use ↗ to open the PDF
Viewer.omitted: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.} other {# more pages not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.}}

ManualEntry.title: Enter this receipt's details
ManualEntry.intro: The AI couldn't read {name}. Type in what's printed on it so you can verify the row.
ManualEntry.summaryPlaceholder: e.g. Paper towels, snack variety pack, folding table

ImageEditor.title: Rotate & crop receipt
ImageEditor.introRestoring: Showing the originally uploaded image. Save to keep it, or Cancel to leave the current one.
ImageEditor.introLocal: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background.
ImageEditor.introStored: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background. Saving replaces the stored image.
ImageEditor.resetTitle: Reset to the originally uploaded image
ImageEditor.loadFailed: Could not load the receipt image
ImageEditor.saveDisabledTitle: Rotate or draw a crop first

Review.title: Review claim
Review.instruction: Check each amount against what you actually paid, pick a ministry, then check it off.
Review.removeDisabledTitle: This is the only receipt — discard the claim instead
Review.removeTitle: Remove receipt from claim (back to the Receipts page)
Review.editPhotoTitle: Rotate or crop this receipt photo
Review.aiFailed: ⚠ The AI couldn't read this receipt.
Review.verifyAllConfirm: Mark {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}} as verified? Check each description, ministry, and amount against its receipt first.
Review.verifyAllButton: Verify {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
Review.profileIncomplete: Your name and mailing address are still missing from your profile — they are printed on the reimbursement form.
Review.amountRestored: Couldn't read that amount — restored the previous value.
Review.descriptionRestored: A description is required — restored the previous text.
Review.esignFinishHint: Finish this claim — print, or e-sign.
Review.revertConfirm: Revert this claim to draft? Only do this if you haven’t turned in the printed form yet. Rows become editable again, and the receipts leave “processed”.
Review.removeConfirm: Remove this receipt from the claim? Its rows are deleted and the receipt goes back to the Receipts page.
Review.discardConfirm: Discard this draft claim? Receipts go back to the Receipts page.
Review.fanOutSet: Set {label} on {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
Review.guideSubtitle: Search by account number or name.
Review.guideEmpty: No category matches — you can still type it under “Other…”.
Review.modeSwitchTitle: Use one ministry for the whole claim?
Review.modeSwitchBody: Every row will be set to {value} — the ministry most rows already use.
Review.modeSwitchBodyNoMinistry: Rows keep no ministry until you pick one at the top.
Review.modeSwitchUnverify: {count, plural, one {# verified row} other {# verified rows}} will need re-verifying.
Review.descPlaceholder: What’s this claim for? e.g. “snacks for the youth retreat”
Review.suggestTitle: Let the AI suggest a ministry & event from your description — you still apply it
Review.noConfidentMatch: No confident match — pick a ministry below.
Review.appliedEveryRow: Applied to every row — you still confirm each amount below.
Review.aiSub: Describe the claim in a sentence — I’ll pick the budget category & event.
Review.candidatesLead: A few of these could fit — which budget line?
Review.somethingElsePlaceholder: Add a detail — e.g. “it was VBS, not a retreat”
Review.pickManuallyEscape: None of these — pick a category below
Review.multiHint: Each row picks its own ministry below. Switch to “One ministry” to set them all at once.
Review.badgeTitle: Ministry applies claim-wide — set it at the top
Review.mergeBlockedTitle: Restore the excluded row before merging
Review.mergeTitle: Merge back into the row above (undo split)
Review.coachHint: One receipt can cover more than one ministry — or include personal items. Use Split on a row to send part of it elsewhere, or leave it off the claim.
Review.nudgeSetAbove: Set the ministry at the top, then verify →
Review.verifyTitle: Click to certify that description, ministry, and amount are correct.
Review.splitAmountQuestion: How much of the {amount} is for something else?
Review.splitForPersonalSub: Not reimbursable — kept off the claim.
Review.splitModeSwitchNote: One ministry is set for the whole claim — assigning a different one here switches it to multiple ministries.
Review.underSignatureFrozen: Under signature — packet and rows are frozen.
Review.revertConfirmSigned: Revert this claim to draft? Every signature collected so far becomes void, and approval starts over.

Esign.withdrawConfirm: Withdraw this submission so you can pick a different approver? You can submit again right after from the claim’s E-sign button.
Esign.checkStatusNoChange: No changes yet — still waiting for {name}.
Esign.checkStatusNoChangePayment: No changes yet — still waiting for payment.
Esign.checkStatusFailed: Couldn't check status — please try again.
Esign.verifyUnavailable: Couldn't check right now — connection problem. Please try again later.
Esign.withdrawRaceApproved: {name} already approved this claim, so it can no longer be withdrawn.
Esign.withdrawRaceRejected: {name} already returned this claim, so there is nothing to withdraw.
Esign.failClosed: Signing is disabled until everything checks out.
Esign.openApprovedPacketButton: 🖋️ Open approved packet — with approver's signature
Esign.notAttested: You can't sign yet — turn on signing on your <link>profile</link>, then get vouched.
Esign.submitIntro: You're asking for approval of this claim ({amount}). Place your signature on the form, pick who should approve it, and sign. If the claim changes later, your signature stops counting and approval starts over.
Esign.prepFailed: Couldn’t load the form — check your connection and try again.
Esign.noEligibleApprovers: No approver is available right now — everyone who could approve is paused or unenrolled. Check with your treasurer.
Esign.placeSignatureHint: Press and hold on the form above to place your signature first.
Esign.typedNameLabel: Type your full name to sign
Esign.consentEnglishNote: The legally binding consent text is the English version below.
Esign.intentAffirmation: I intend this to be my signature.
Esign.connectBody: Sign in with your church Google account to sign securely. You only need to do this once on this device.
Esign.connectBlocked: Your browser blocked the sign-in window. Tap “Connect signing” again to allow it.
Esign.bannerOk: ✓ Everything checks out — this is the genuine paperwork, unchanged.
Esign.bannerBad: ✗ Something doesn't check out. Don't sign — ask the person who sent this (details below for whoever helps you).
Esign.anomaliesHeader: {count, plural, one {# invalid event on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):} other {# invalid events on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):}}
Esign.independentCheck: Independent check: download the certificate and run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs with the church's published root fingerprint.
Esign.signedDragHint: Signed. Drag your signature if you want it somewhere else.
Esign.holdTabHint: Press and hold the yellow tab to sign on the line.
Esign.appearsAfterTap: Your signature appears after you hold the yellow tab.
Esign.couldNotOpenDoc: Couldn't open the document to sign. {message}
Esign.signAbove: Sign above with your finger or mouse
Esign.waitingFor: Waiting for {name} to review this claim.
Esign.approverPausedNote: {name} isn't taking new approval requests right now. They can still decide this claim, or you can withdraw it and pick a different approver.
Esign.approverIneligibleNote: {name} can no longer approve claims. Withdraw this submission and pick a different approver.
Esign.approverPrefilledFrom: Pre-filled from the {position} default. Change it below if needed.
Esign.signedButNotSynced: Your signature was recorded. The page couldn’t refresh the claim’s status — it will catch up on its own. Do not sign again.
Esign.consentGloss: In short: you agree that signing in this app counts as your real, legally binding signature — the same as ink on paper. (This is a summary; the binding text is the English document below.)
Esign.withdrawFailed: Could not withdraw the submission — nothing changed. Try again.
Esign.reviseConfirm: Reopen this claim for editing? Every signature collected so far becomes void, and approval starts over when you resubmit.
Esign.waitingDays: · waiting {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
Esign.alreadyDecidedNote: This claim has already been decided — the list will update in a moment.
Esign.assignedElsewhereNote: This claim is assigned to a different approver.

Approvals.title: Approvals
Approvals.subtitle: Open a claim to check it and sign.
Approvals.rejectNeedsComment: Add a comment explaining the rejection
Approvals.pausedNotice: You've turned off new approval requests. These claims were assigned to you before that — please decide them, or their owners can reassign them.
Approvals.roleLostNotice: Your approver role has been removed. You can decline these claims so their owners can reassign them, but you can't approve them.
Approvals.rejectConfirmBody: Rejecting is final — you won't be able to undo it or approve this claim later. {name} will have to revise and resubmit the claim for a new approval.
Approvals.openGone: That claim isn’t in this list anymore — it may already have been decided.

Finance.title: Finance
Finance.subtitle: Approved claims ready to pay.
Finance.selectedCount: {count, plural, one {# packet selected} other {# packets selected}}
Finance.printFailed: Couldn't build the packets. Please try again.
Finance.openGone: That claim isn’t in this list anymore — it may already have been paid.
Finance.noCheckNumberNote: No check number — that’s fine for non-check payments; add one if a check was written.

Identity.title: Electronic signing
Identity.switchOnBody: Members can enroll, submit, approve, and pay electronically.
Identity.switchOffBody: Off for everyone (the default). Existing signed records stay verifiable.
Identity.bootstrapIntro: You're the person who sets up electronic signing for this church. It starts switched off, so nothing changes for members until you turn it on.
Identity.notSetUp: Electronic signing isn't set up for this church yet — ask your administrator.
Identity.enrollIntro: Sign and approve reimbursements from your phone — no printing. You'll agree to sign electronically, draw your signature, and then two members confirm it's really you (in person, one quick scan).
Identity.pendingVouch: One last step: show this to two members (or one approver) in person. They open their Vouch tab, scan it, and confirm it's really you.
Identity.manualFallbackBody: Have your voucher pick you from the list on their Vouch screen and type this key fingerprint by hand:
Identity.consentTitle: Sign electronically instead of on paper
Identity.consentIntro: Please read this once — it says your electronic signature counts like an ink one, and that you can always go back to paper.
Identity.drawIntro: This is the signature that appears on the reimbursement forms — sign the way you would on paper, with your finger or the mouse.
Identity.redrawBody: Your new signature is used on everything you sign from now on. Nothing you've already signed changes.
Identity.allowlistOnMembersPage: Choose who can use electronic signing on the <link>Members</link> page. Removing someone hides signing from them; anything they already signed stays valid.
Identity.scanFromVouchTab: Important: they should scan this from the Vouch tab inside this app — not with their phone’s camera app.
Identity.whoCanVouch: Members who can vouch for you: {names}{more, plural, =0 {} other { and # more}}.
Identity.revokedBody: Your signing key was retired by an administrator. Everything you signed before stays valid, but to sign again you’ll need to set up signing afresh and be vouched in person again.

Devices.newDeviceTitle: This looks like a new device.
Devices.newDeviceBody: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this device yet. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed.
Devices.newDeviceBodyGone: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this account anymore. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed unless you start over.
Devices.approveFromOther: 📱 Approve from a device you already sign with
Devices.keysRemovedEverywhere: Your signing keys were removed everywhere, so the only way forward is to start over below.
Devices.noneWorkBody: If your old devices are gone and you never printed a recovery sheet, your identity can't be recovered — that's what keeps it yours. Starting over creates a brand-new signing key: the same people vouch for you again, in person, and the moment they do, your old key stops counting on its own. Worried someone might use the old key before then? Tell your administrator and they can retire it right away.
Devices.startOverConfirm:
Start over from nothing?

This signs ALL your devices out and throws away your current signing key. You'll set up again from scratch and be vouched for again in person — which automatically retires the old key. Anything you already signed stays valid.
Devices.waitingIntro: Now pick up the device you already sign with. A message appears there — type this code into it:
Devices.waitingNote: Waiting for the other device… this page continues by itself once it's approved.
Devices.phraseWrong: That phrase didn't work — check the words and their order
Devices.approveHint: Only approve if it's yours and in your hands. Type the 6-digit code from its screen — that's what proves it's really that device.
Devices.devicesTitle: Devices that can sign as you
Devices.removeConfirm:
Sign “{name}” out of electronic signing?

It loses access right away. If the device might be in someone else's hands, tell your administrator in person too, so they can revoke your signing key.
Devices.lostHint: Lost one? Remove it here, then tell your administrator in person if someone else might have it — they can revoke the signing key itself.
Devices.recoveryRoot: Protect the church's signing anchor. If this device is lost with no recovery sheet, electronic signing has to be rebuilt from scratch for everyone.
Devices.recoveryNudge: One more safety net: print a recovery sheet and you can sign from a new device even if this one is lost. Without one, losing your devices means starting over and being vouched in person again.
Devices.sheetBody: This one-page sheet holds the 24 words that can bring your signing identity to a new device. Print it and keep it with your important papers at home. Anyone holding the sheet can sign as you — and nobody, including the church, can recreate it if it's lost.
Devices.noPrinter: No printer? Copy the words by hand
Devices.savedCheckbox: The sheet is printed (or written out) and stored somewhere safe.
Devices.rejectFailed: Could not reject the request — it may still be pending. Try again.
Devices.freshPhraseNote: Opening this creates a brand-new phrase — any sheet you printed earlier stops working.

Vouch.title: Vouch for a member
Vouch.onlyAttested: Only attested members can vouch. Enable signing on your <link>profile</link> and get vouched first.
Vouch.doneBody: They need two member vouches (or one from an approver) to become attested.
Vouch.beforeTitle: Before you vouch, confirm in person:
Vouch.bullet1: The person is physically with you right now.
Vouch.bullet2: This name and email really belong to them.
Vouch.bullet3: You scanned the QR from THEIR screen (not a forwarded link).
Vouch.rekeyBody: Usually that means a lost device. The moment they're vouched back in, the old key stops counting — everything they signed before stays valid. Be extra sure it's really them.
Vouch.manualLabel: Type their full key fingerprint (from their screen — the spoken 6-digit code is never enough):
Vouch.confirmLabel: I am with {name} in person and confirm this identity is theirs.
Vouch.scanIntro: Scan the candidate's QR right here with your camera. Can't use the camera? Pick them from the list and type their full key fingerprint (read from their screen; the short spoken code is not enough).
Vouch.orPick: Or pick them and type their fingerprint
Vouch.malformedLink: This vouch link is malformed — rescan the QR
Vouch.membersPageHint: Looking for the member roster? Roles, signing keys, and e-sign access are managed on the <link>Members</link> page.
Vouch.keylessDevice: This browser doesn’t hold your signing key yet, so vouching from here will fail. Add this device from your Profile first, or vouch from a device you already sign on.

VouchScan.aim: Point at the candidate's QR code
VouchScan.notVouch: That's not a vouch code — line up the candidate's QR code.
VouchScan.insecure: This device can't open the camera here. Use the list below instead.
VouchScan.denied: Camera access was blocked. Allow the camera for this app in your device settings, or use the list below.
VouchScan.startFailed: Couldn't start the camera. Use the list below instead.

Verify.linkInvalid: This verification link is not valid or has expired
Verify.storyGeneric: This paperwork was cryptographically checked in your browser — see the details below.

story.paid: {name} asked to be paid back {amount}. A different, authorized person approved it, and the church treasurer paid it — and the paperwork you're looking at hasn't changed since it was signed.
story.approved: {name} asked to be paid back {amount}. A different, authorized person approved it. The treasurer hasn't recorded payment yet, and the paperwork hasn't changed since it was signed.
story.submitted: {name} asked to be paid back {amount} and signed the request. It's waiting for an authorized approver — and nothing has changed since it was signed.
story.rejected: {name} asked to be paid back {amount}, and an authorized approver reviewed it and asked for changes. The paperwork itself is genuine and unchanged.
story.warn: Most of this checks out, but one thing doesn't add up — check the details below before treating this as the genuine paperwork.

Verify.checkedNow: Checked on your device just now — not taken on trust from a server.
Verify.peopleHint: Tap anyone to see who confirmed their identity, in person — then keep opening to follow the chain up to the church's root. Links deeper than the direct vouchers are shown by their key, not their name.
Verify.rootCaption: The trust anchor set when the church first opened its records. You confirm this one identity out-of-band — the key shown here matches the fingerprint printed in the church's records.
Verify.docLeadOk: These pages are the approved copy.
Verify.docBodyOk: The final, stamped version — safe to print and file. Your phone confirmed it's built from the original everyone signed, unchanged.
Verify.docLeadWarn: This copy no longer matches what was signed.
Verify.docBodyWarn: Your phone found that these pages were changed after they were signed. Don't treat them as the genuine paperwork.
Verify.stepsTitle: Step by step — and what each step sealed
Verify.stepSubmit: The form was filled out and signed
Verify.howSubmit: Made with {name}'s own signing key — the identity vouched for above, kept on their device — so this is their signature, not just a typed name.
Verify.howApprove: They reviewed the very same document that was signed, and they are authorized to approve.
Verify.howPaid: Only the church treasurer can mark a claim paid.
Verify.howPaidCheck: Only the church treasurer can mark a claim paid. Recorded as check #{number}.
Verify.vcapOriginal: Signing the form sealed the original — the one version every later signature points to. It's frozen here and can never change.
Verify.vcapApproved: Approving sealed a copy: the original plus the approver's stamp, with the original's mark printed on it — so the stamp can't be moved onto another document.
Verify.vcapNone: The payment didn't create another document — it points back to the approved copy above.
Verify.lockLine: Each step seals its own copy the moment it happens, and every later copy is built from the original and carries its mark. So no page, and no step, can be edited, removed, or swapped afterward.
Verify.rootRelayed: root relayed by this server (verify the fingerprint in person)
Verify.packetMatch: packet bytes match the signed hash ({hash}…)
Verify.packetMismatch: packet bytes do NOT match the signed hash
Verify.anomalyCount: {count, plural, one {# ledger anomaly} other {# ledger anomalies}}
Verify.rosterOddities: {count, plural, one {# roster oddity} other {# roster oddities}}
Verify.independentCheckCert: Independent check: run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs against the certificate's embedded bundle with the church's published root fingerprint.

Errors.verifiedEmailRequired: A verified email address is required
Errors.receiptOnClaim: Receipt is part of a claim and cannot be deleted
Errors.previewPdfOnly: Preview is only available for PDF receipts
Errors.imageEditImagesOnly: Only image receipts can be rotated or cropped
Errors.receiptFrozenByClaim: This receipt is on a generated claim — revert that claim before editing the image
Errors.nothingToRestore: This receipt has no earlier version to restore
Errors.cropTooSmall: Crop region is too small to stay legible
Errors.claimFrozen: Claim already generated; line items are frozen
Errors.ministryRequiredToVerify: Choose a ministry before verifying this row
Errors.splitAmountsInvalid: Split amounts must be non-zero and sum to the original amount
Errors.nothingToMergeInto: No row above from the same receipt to merge into
Errors.restoreBeforeMerge: Restore the excluded row before merging
Errors.claimSettingsFrozen: Claim already generated; review settings are frozen
Errors.onlyDraftDeletable: Only draft claims can be deleted
Errors.receiptsNotFound: One or more receipts were not found
Errors.claimReceiptsFrozen: Claim already generated; receipts are frozen
Errors.receiptsAlreadyOnClaim: One or more receipts are already on this claim
Errors.claimEmpty: Claim has no line items to reimburse
Errors.rowsUnverified: {count} row(s) still need verification before the PDF can be generated
Errors.rowsMissingMinistry: {count} row(s) still need a ministry before the PDF can be generated
Errors.profileIncomplete: Add your name and mailing address to your profile first — they are printed on the reimbursement form
Errors.receiptNotOnClaim: Receipt not found in this claim
Errors.rowAlreadyEdited: This receipt's row has already been edited — adjust it directly
Errors.lastReceiptOnClaim: This is the only receipt in the claim — discard the claim instead
Errors.onlyGeneratedRevertible: Only generated or under-signature claims can be reverted to draft
Errors.descriptionRequired: Describe the claim in a sentence first
Errors.aiRateLimited: The AI provider is rate-limited right now — try again in a minute or pick a ministry manually
Errors.aiNoSuggestion: The AI couldn't produce a suggestion — pick a ministry manually
Errors.aiQuotaExhausted: The AI provider is busy right now — please wait a minute and try again. Affected: {names}
Errors.quotaWait: Rate limit reached — waiting {seconds}s before retrying ({attempt}/{maxRetries})…
Errors.pdfFrozen: The packet is frozen under signature — download it from the claim page, or revert to draft to edit

esign.pickApprover: Pick an approver and type your name
esign.placeSignature: Place your signature on the form first
esign.typeNameApproval: Type your name to sign an approval
esign.selfApprove: You cannot approve your own claim
esign.notApprover: That member is not an attested approver
esign.packetChanged: The packet changed since preflight — restart the ceremony
esign.generateFirst: Generate the packet PDF before submitting
esign.mirrorBehind: Ledger mirror is behind — reconcile this claim first
esign.noPacket: No packet for this claim yet
esign.certNotReady: Certificates exist once a claim is approved
esign.notSetUp: E-sign is not set up yet
esign.notAllowed: Electronic signing is not enabled for your account
esign.approverUnavailable: That approver isn't taking new approval requests right now — pick someone else
esign.approverRoleLost: Your approver role has been removed — you can decline, but not approve
esign.decisionNotBinding: The signed decision doesn't bind on the ledger — reload this claim and try again, or ask its owner to reassign it

admin.contextTooLong: The church context is too long (max {max} bytes).
admin.unknownField: That setting can’t be edited here.

ministry.invalid: Those budget categories couldn’t be saved — check the form and try again.
ministry.codeFormat: Account code must be exactly 3 digits and not 999: “{code}”.
ministry.codeDuplicate: Two active categories share the code {code} — codes must be unique.

Errors.embeddingProbeFailed: Could not reach the embedding server. Check the address and key, or use “Save without testing”.

position.invalid: Those positions couldn’t be saved — check the form and try again.
position.unknownHolder: One of the selected people couldn’t be found. Refresh and try again.

team.invalid: Those teams couldn’t be saved — check the form and try again.
team.unknownMember: One of the selected people couldn’t be found. Refresh and try again.

Errors.searchInvalid: That search couldn’t be understood — adjust it and try again
Errors.searchEmptyQuery: Type something to search for first
Errors.admin.selfLockout: This change would remove your own admin access — keep your email in the list

AddReceipts.title: Add receipts to this claim
AddReceipts.intro: Pick from your existing receipts or upload new ones — each is read with AI and gets its own row to verify.
AddReceipts.emptyBody: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to add it.
AddReceipts.readingCount: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
AddReceipts.confirmCount: ✨ Add {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}

Admin.title: Admin
Admin.subtitle: Configure the app, watch usage, and review the roster.
Admin.health_aiMock: AI is in mock mode — extraction and suggestions use canned data, not a real provider.
Admin.health_aiNoKey: No API key set for the {provider} AI provider ({keyVar}). Receipt extraction will fail.
Admin.health_aiErrors: Failed AI calls in the last 24 hours: {count}. See the Logs tab.
Admin.health_contextMissing: No church context document yet — ministry suggestions use the chart of accounts alone.
Admin.health_publicUrlUnset: Public web address not set — generated PDFs won’t carry a QR link.
Admin.health_firebaseIncomplete: Firebase sign-in isn’t fully configured — Google sign-in is unavailable.
Admin.health_esignNotSetUp: Electronic signatures aren’t set up yet.
Admin.health_esignDisabled: Electronic signatures are set up but currently turned off.
Admin.health_claimsStuck: Claims awaiting approval more than {days} days: {count}.
Admin.moreInUsage: More detail in the Usage tab.
Admin.contextIntro: Names of small groups, recurring events, and labeling rules the chart of accounts can’t resolve. It is added to every “Suggest ministry” call and applies immediately after saving.
Admin.contextPrivacy: Everything here is sent to your AI provider whenever someone clicks Suggest. Think twice before naming individuals.
Admin.contextSavedNote: Saved. New suggestions use this right away.
Admin.settingsIntro: Deployment settings. Changes are written to the data volume and picked up without a restart.
Admin.aiChartAria: Daily AI call volume over the last 30 days
Admin.esignNotSetUpAdmin: Not set up yet. The root account sets this up from their Profile.
Admin.esignOnBody: Members can enroll, sign, and approve.
Admin.esignOffBody: Signing is paused; existing signatures still verify.
Admin.membersManageNote: Roles, signing keys, and e-sign access are managed on the <link>Members</link> page.
Admin.chainIntro: The signing roster, verified from the ledger in your browser — who is attested, their roles, and pending vouches.
Admin.chainNeedsEsign: Turn on electronic signatures to see the roster.
Admin.chainNeedsEnroll: Enroll to sign (Profile) to load the roster in this browser.
Admin.chainPending: {name} — {vouches} vouch(es) so far (needs 2, or one from an approver+).
Admin.fields_AI_PROVIDER_help: Which service runs receipt extraction and the “Suggest ministry” calls. Switch anytime — it applies to the next claim built or suggestion; past extractions and their logs are untouched. Fill in the matching API key below.
Admin.fields_OPENROUTER_API_KEY_help: Your OpenRouter key, used only while the provider above is OpenRouter. Stored on the server and never shown again — leave blank to keep the current one.
Admin.fields_OPENROUTER_MODEL_help: The OpenRouter model id used for extraction and suggestions when OpenRouter is the provider. Blank uses the default, google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite.
Admin.fields_GEMINI_API_KEY_help: Your Google AI Studio (Gemini) key, used only while the provider above is Google. Stored on the server and never shown again — leave blank to keep the current one.
Admin.fields_GEMINI_MODEL_help: The Gemini model id used when Google is the provider. Blank uses the default, gemini-3.1-flash-lite.
Admin.fields_AI_RPM_TARGET_help: Caps how many provider calls per minute the server makes, so a batch of receipts stays under your plan’s limit (Gemini’s free tier is 15/min). Extra calls wait their turn instead of failing. No effect while AI is mocked. Default 15.
Admin.fields_AI_QUOTA_COOLDOWN_MS_help: After a rate-limit error, how long to wait before retrying, in milliseconds. The wait is shown to whoever is building the claim. Default 60000 (one minute).
Admin.fields_AI_QUOTA_MAX_RETRIES_help: How many times to retry after a rate-limit error before giving up (0 surfaces it at once). Applies to receipt extraction; the interactive Suggest call never waits. Default 3.
Admin.fields_PUBLIC_BASE_URL_help: This deployment’s public web address. When set, generated PDFs carry a QR code back to the claim packet and signature certificates link to it. A reverse proxy hides the real URL, so it’s explicit here. Changing it only affects PDFs generated afterward; it’s also required for first-party sign-in below.
Admin.fields_ESIGN_ROOT_EMAIL_help: The one account allowed to run the one-time e-signature setup. This matters only BEFORE setup: once e-signatures are established the root identity is fixed in the signed genesis record, so changing this afterward does nothing (re-rooting is a separate re-genesis ceremony, not an email change).
Admin.fields_ESIGN_ROOT_FINGERPRINT_help: Optional out-of-band fingerprint of the root signing key. When set, this app and every verifier reject any roster whose root key doesn’t match — the guard against a swapped trust anchor. Set it before setup to pin the expected root; afterward it must equal the established root’s fingerprint or verification fails closed.
Admin.fields_FIREBASE_API_KEY_help: From your Firebase web-app config. This is a public client identifier (every browser receives it), shown here so you can verify it. Google sign-in appears only when this, the auth domain, and the project ID are all set.
Admin.fields_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN_help: From your Firebase web-app config — usually your-project.firebaseapp.com. The sign-in domain in the normal flow; if you enable first-party sign-in below, point it at your own domain instead. A full https:// URL is accepted (only the host is used).
Admin.fields_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID_help: Your Firebase project id. Used both in the browser sign-in config and on the server to verify the Google ID tokens users present — so it must match the project your users actually sign in to. It also derives the first-party sign-in target.
Admin.fields_FIREBASE_APP_ID_help: Optional, from the Firebase web-app config. Sign-in works without it; include it only if your setup requires it.
Admin.fields_FIREBASE_AUTH_PROXY_help: Serve Google sign-in from this app’s own domain instead of firebaseapp.com — fixes iOS/Safari sign-in failures. Needs the public web address above, plus the redirect URI and authorized domain registered in the Firebase console.
Admin.fields_FIREBASE_AUTH_UPSTREAM_HOST_help: Advanced. The host the first-party sign-in proxy forwards to. Blank derives it from the project id (project.firebaseapp.com), which is right for normal projects — set this only for unusual Firebase setups.
Admin.fields_ADMIN_EMAILS_help: Emails that get admin access to this area, comma-separated — the way to add admins without the e-sign roster. It’s app-access only and never changes anyone’s signing role. Takes effect immediately; keep your own email here so you don’t lock yourself out.

search.actionFailed: That didn't work. Please try again.
search.statusUpToDate: Search is up to date — {indexed} items indexed.
search.memberPreview: Members currently see: “Search is still indexing older items — about {count} to go.”
search.connectIntro: Search lets members find receipts and claims by describing them. Enter the two values from whoever runs your church's AI server.
search.envHint: The server's environment sets a different endpoint — since first setup, this page is what counts.
search.modelWarning: Identifies this search index — changing it rebuilds search from scratch (several hours).
search.skipProbeLabel: Save without testing (server is down and I know it)
search.savedRebuilding: Saved — the search index is rebuilding in the background.

failedClass.unreadableImage: The search server couldn't read this {kind}'s image. It can still be found by its text (exact match), just not by description.
failedClass.tooLong: This {kind} is too large for the search server to read in full. It can still be found by its text.
failedClass.endpointDown: The search server couldn't be reached when this {kind} was processed. Retry once it's back.
failedClass.generic: Search couldn't index this {kind}. It can still be found by its text (exact match).

search.testQueryPlaceholder: e.g. folding tables for the retreat
search.rebuildHint: Re-reads everything on the current model — use if the search server was changed out from under this app.
search.confirmModelChange: Changing the model erases every stored embedding and re-reads all receipts and claims against the AI endpoint — search stays degraded for several hours. Continue?
search.confirmRebuild: Rebuild the whole search index? Every receipt and claim is re-read against the AI endpoint — this takes hours and uses AI calls.

Ministries.title: Budget categories
Ministries.subtitle: The list requesters pick from, and the descriptions that guide them. Church-wide.
Ministries.loadDefaultsTitle: Add any missing built-in categories and fill blank descriptions with the built-in guidance. Nothing is overwritten, and nothing is saved until you save.
Ministries.defaultsLoaded: Loaded the built-in defaults — {added, plural, =0 {no new categories} one {# new category} other {# new categories}} added, {filled, plural, =0 {no blank descriptions} one {# blank description} other {# blank descriptions}} filled in. Review the changes, then save.
Ministries.defaultsNoop: Nothing to load — every built-in category is already in the list, and no blank description matched one.
Ministries.name: Name
Ministries.descriptionPlaceholder: Add a hint for requesters — it also guides the AI’s category suggestions…
Ministries.renameWarning: Renaming or recoding a category that’s already on claims won’t relabel those past rows — their printed value stays as it was. To retire one, archive it instead.
Ministries.reservedNote: Codes are 3 digits. 999 is reserved for uncategorized “Other…” entries and never prints on the form.
Ministries.empty: No categories yet — add one to get started.
Ministries.routesNobody: No holder here can approve yet — claims won't be pre-filled.
Ministries.routesNoHolder: This position has no holder yet.

Search.title: Search
Search.searchFailed: Search didn't go through. Please try again.
Search.scopeDecidedHint: Claims you approved or rejected — not ones still waiting on you. Leave the search box empty to list them all.
Search.emptyBody: Try fewer or different words — exact wording was searched too.
Search.pendingReceipts: {count, plural, one {1 of your receipts is still being added to search — usually ready in about a minute.} other {# of your receipts are still being added to search — usually ready in about a minute.}}
Search.pendingClaims: {count, plural, one {A claim you edited recently will appear in search a few minutes after you stop editing.} other {# claims you edited recently will appear in search a few minutes after you stop editing.}}
Search.pendingRebuild: Search is still indexing older items — about {count} to go.
Search.resultsAnnounce: {count, plural, =0 {No results} one {# result} other {# results}}
Search.degraded: Right now search only finds results containing your exact words. Descriptive search will be back soon.
Search.dateHint: Search matches descriptions, not dates — dates are shown on each result.
Search.staleNote: Filters changed — search again to update.
Search.scopeTeamHint: Receipts and claims under your teams' budget categories. Leave the search empty to list all of them.
Search.viewOnlyCard: View only — you don’t have access to open this claim.
Search.emptyDegradedBody: Descriptive search is temporarily unavailable and nothing matched the exact wording — try again shortly.
Search.emptyTeamBrowse: Your teams have no receipts on submitted claims yet — new ones will appear here.
Search.unavailableBody: An administrator needs to configure the search backend before this page works. Everything else in the app is unaffected.

Positions.title: Positions
Positions.subtitle: Custom approval roles you assign to people and use as the default approver for budget categories.
Positions.authorityNote: A position is a routing label, not authority. Assigning someone here does not let them approve — they still need the Approver role, granted through the signature roster on the <link>Members</link> page.
Positions.empty: No positions yet — add one to set default approvers for your budget categories.
Positions.loadDefaultsHint: Adds the standard deacon roles, ready for you to assign people and save.
Positions.deleteConfirm: Delete “{name}”? This permanently removes the position.
Positions.deleteConfirmInUse: It is the default approver for {count, plural, one {# budget category} other {# budget categories}} — that default will be cleared.
Positions.usedByNone: Not used by any budget category
Positions.holderPausedWarn: {name} has paused approvals, so claims won't pre-fill to them until they resume.
Positions.holderCannotWarn: {name} can't approve until an executive officer or admin grants them the Approver role on the <link>Members</link> page.
Positions.routingNote: A claim that spans several budget categories pre-fills the approver from its largest-dollar category’s default; unavailable holders are skipped.
Positions.localizedNamesHint: Chinese names are optional — the English name is used where one is blank.

Members.title: Members
Members.subtitle: Roles, positions, e-sign enrollment, and access for everyone in the church — managed in one place.
Members.esignOffNote: Electronic signing is turned off right now, so enrollment status and access controls are hidden here.
Members.othersEmpty: Everyone in the directory has been attested.
Members.pendingHint: People awaiting vouches become attested in person — see <link>Vouch for a member</link>.
Members.roleDialogFromTo: This changes their role from {from} to {to}.
Members.roleExplainMember: Members can prepare and sign their own reimbursements, but can't approve or pay others'.
Members.roleExplainApprover: Approvers can approve reimbursement claims for payment.
Members.roleExplainSecretary: The secretary is an executive officer of the board: they can approve reimbursement claims and change members' roles.
Members.roleExplainChairman: The chairman is an executive officer of the board: they can approve reimbursement claims and change members' roles.
Members.roleExplainTreasurer: Treasurers can approve claims and mark them paid, and as an executive officer can change members' roles.
Members.keyDialogBody: Do this only when a device that could sign as them was lost or stolen. Everything they've already signed stays valid, but the key stops working everywhere — they'll set up signing again and get vouched back in person.
Members.statusRevokedHint: Their signing key was retired. To sign again they must set up signing afresh on their Profile and be vouched in person.
Members.statusNoneHint: They haven’t set up electronic signing. It starts from their own Profile page.

Teams.title: Teams
Teams.subtitle: Groups of members who can view the receipts filed under their budget categories.
Teams.readOnlyNote: Team membership is view-only. Members can see, list, and search receipts on filed claims under the team's budget categories — they can't edit anything, and drafts stay private to their owners.
Teams.empty: No teams yet — add one to share budget-category receipts with its members.
Teams.noMinistries: No budget categories yet — members won't see anything until one is added.
Teams.archiveEndsAccess: Archiving ends every member’s read access when you save.
